text:
CT CHEST, ABDOMEN, AND PELVIS WITH IV CONTRAST
End-of-Treatment Restaging Examination

CLINICAL INDICATION: 66-year-old female with history of borderline resectable pancreatic head adenocarcinoma (T4 N1 M0 at diagnosis), treated with neoadjuvant modified FOLFIRINOX (8 cycles), consolidative long-course chemoradiation (50.4 Gy with concurrent cape), followed by pylorus-preserving pancreaticoduodenectomy (Whipple procedure) achieving R0 resection (ypT2 ypN0, CAP regression score 2), and subsequent adjuvant Gemzar/Xeloda (6 cycles, recently completed). Examination requested for end-of-treatment disease restaging. Prior postoperative fluid collection near pancreaticojejunostomy anastomosis was noted on earlier surveillance imaging. CA 19-9 most recently 21 U/mL.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed using a pancreatic protocol acquisition. Images were obtained during the arterial, portal venous, and delayed phases following intravenous administration of iohexol contrast (100 mL at 4 mL/sec). Coronal and sagittal multiplanar reformats were generated and reviewed. CT chest performed in the standard fashion without dedicated contrast timing for pulmonary vasculature.

COMPARISON: Prior CT abdomen/pelvis performed approximately 4 months earlier (cycle 3 adjuvant therapy surveillance scan); CT chest from same prior examination.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways: The lungs are clear bilaterally. No pulmonary nodules, masses, or consolidations are identified. No ground-glass opacities. The airways are patent to the segmental level. No endobronchial lesions.

Pleural Spaces: No pleural effusion. No pneumothorax.

Mediastinum and Hila: The mediastinum is midline. Cardiac silhouette is within normal limits for technique. No pericardial effusion. No mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy. The visualized thoracic aorta is of normal caliber. Superior vena cava is unremarkable.

Chest Wall and Osseous Structures: No suspicious lytic or blastic lesions in the imaged ribs, sternum, or thoracic vertebral bodies. No pathologic fractures.

---

ABDOMEN AND PELVIS:

Surgical Bed / Pancreatic Region:
Post-operative changes from the prior pylorus-preserving pancreaticoduodenectomy are again demonstrated. The pancreatic head and uncinate process are absent, consistent with the surgical resection. The remaining pancreatic body and tail appear preserved with expected mild parenchymal atrophy, without focal mass lesion or suspicious enhancing nodularity. The main pancreatic duct in the remnant pancreas measures approximately 3 mm in the body — mildly prominent, likely a post-surgical baseline finding for this patient.

The pancreaticojejunostomy anastomosis is intact in appearance. Previously described simple fluid collection adjacent to the anastomotic site, which measured 2.8 cm on the prior examination, is no longer identified on the current study. The region demonstrates expected post-surgical soft tissue stranding and fibrosis without discrete fluid pocket, mass, or abnormal enhancement. No gas locules to suggest fistulous tract or abscess. The mesenteric fat at the surgical bed demonstrates expected mature fibrotic change, with no nodular or enhancing soft tissue components to raise concern for local tumor recurrence.

Hepatic and Biliary:
The liver is normal in size and contour. The hepatic parenchyma is homogeneous in attenuation throughout all phases. No focal hepatic lesions are identified — specifically, no hypoenhancing nodules, arterially enhancing foci, or delayed washout lesions are present. The intrahepatic bile ducts are not dilated. The hepaticojejunostomy anastomosis is patent with no upstream intrahepatic ductal dilation. The gallbladder is surgically absent. No biloma.

Spleen: Normal size and homogeneous enhancement. No focal lesions. No splenomegaly.

Adrenal Glands: Both adrenal glands are normal in size and morphology. No masses or nodules identified bilaterally.

Kidneys: Both kidneys enhance symmetrically and excrete contrast promptly. No hydronephrosis. No renal masses. A small cortical cyst in the right kidney measuring approximately 8 mm is noted, simple in morphology, incidental.

Vascular Structures: The superior mesenteric artery (SMA) is patent with no significant wall thickening or irregularity at the prior tumor contact site. The superior mesenteric vein (SMV) and portal vein are patent with no filling defect or thrombosis. The celiac axis and its branches are unremarkable. The abdominal aorta and inferior vena cava are within normal limits.

Lymph Nodes: No pathologically enlarged mesenteric, retroperitoneal, or pelvic lymph nodes are present. The largest visible retroperitoneal node measures approximately 7 mm in short axis — within the range of a normal reactive node. No confluent nodal masses. No para-aortic, aortocaval, or common iliac adenopathy.

Peritoneum and Omentum: No peritoneal thickening, nodularity, or implants. The omentum is unremarkable without evidence of omental caking. No ascites.

Bowel: The gastrojejunostomy and duodenojejunostomy reconstruction are intact. The bowel loops are otherwise unremarkable. No obstruction, ileus, or wall thickening to suggest recurrence or radiation-induced enteritis at this time. No pneumoperitoneum.

Pelvis: The uterus and adnexa appear atrophic, appropriate for patient age. No pelvic masses. Urinary bladder is adequately distended and unremarkable. No free fluid in the pelvis.

Bone (imaged): No lytic or sclerotic lesions identified in the imaged lumbar spine, sacrum, or pelvic bones. Mild multilevel lumbar degenerative disc changes are noted, age-appropriate.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. No imaging evidence of locoregional recurrence. The surgical bed at the prior pancreaticoduodenectomy site demonstrates expected post-treatment fibrotic change only. No enhancing nodularity, soft tissue mass, or other morphologic feature to suggest residual or recurrent tumor in the pancreatic region or along the SMA/SMV margin.

2. No hepatic metastases. The liver is clear throughout all contrast phases without any focal lesions.

3. No pulmonary nodules or masses. The chest is clear bilaterally.

4. Complete resolution of the previously identified anastomotic fluid collection adjacent to the pancreaticojejunostomy. This area now shows only expected mature fibrosis.

5. No abdominal or pelvic lymphadenopathy. No peritoneal disease or ascites.

6. Patent SMA and SMV with no vascular complications at the surgical site.

7. Overall, the findings are consistent with the expected post-operative and post-treatment appearance following multimodality therapy for pancreatic adenocarcinoma. Correlated with the reported CA 19-9 of 21 U/mL (within normal limits), the overall picture is reassuring for no detectable disease at this time.
